pattern: The target string is fixed the moment you compute it, so the minimum number of flips is just a plain position-by-position comparison between the original bits and that fixed reversed copy.
hints: The string you must match, the reverse of the original, never changes while you are flipping bits -- it is computed once from the original binary form and stays fixed.
So this is not about turning the string into a palindrome of itself; it is a direct comparison between the original string and that one fixed target string, position by position.
Count every index where the original bit differs from the target bit at that same index; that count is the answer, and it naturally counts both sides of a mismatched mirrored pair separately.

edge_inputs.description: already a palindrome, no flips needed
a power of two, binary form is 1 followed by all zeros
one less than a power of two, binary form is all ones
an odd-length binary string whose middle bit needs no partner, but outer bits mismatch
only one mismatched pair among several matching pairs
an even-length binary string with several mismatched pairs

stress.description: random n across the full 32-bit range
